nativeRange.habitat: Still or slow-moving freshwater bodies including ponds, lakes, ditches and slow rivers; tolerates a wide temperature range from cool temperate to tropical

decorPreferences: open surface area for floating growth
optional anchor points or weights if used as a background plant

goodWith: cold-water and pond fish such as goldfish
avoidWith: strongly herbivorous fish such as silver dollars or large plecos
very high-flow tanks that shred the brittle stems

summary: Hornwort (Ceratophyllum demersum) is an extremely hardy, fast-growing rootless stem plant. It is commonly grown floating or loosely anchored, tolerates cold to tropical conditions, and is widely reported to suppress algae by competing for nutrients and releasing allelopathic compounds.
careNotes: Ceratophyllum demersum is a cosmopolitan rootless aquatic plant and one of the hardiest options for freshwater aquaria and ponds. It has no true roots, so it is most commonly floated at the surface where access to light is best, but it can also be loosely tied to driftwood, weighted, or pushed into the substrate as a background plant where it forms a fluffy underwater bush. Reported height in aquariums is roughly 30–80 cm under typical conditions, with very fast growth (commonly 3–10 cm per week under good light). It is widely regarded as a useful nutrient sponge and is repeatedly described as algicidal/allelopathic — Flowgrow notes it 'produces a natural algae growth inhibitor', and Modern Aquarium credits it with reducing algae by competing for excess nutrients. CO2 injection is not required; lighting can range from low to high (Tropica rates light demand as low; Flowgrow as medium–high; Aquarium Co-Op notes it tolerates the full range, growing fastest as a floater under brighter light). Cross-source disagreement on lighting was averaged toward 'medium' as a representative middle ground. Temperature tolerance is wide: aquarium sources converge on roughly 15–28°C as a comfortable range, with the plant surviving 10–30°C and even down to ~4°C in outdoor ponds — making it a strong choice for unheated tanks, coldwater fish (goldfish), and temperate ponds. pH tolerance is broad (Flowgrow reports 6–9); the conservative aquarium-typical range of 6.0–7.5 is used. The plant is brittle and tends to shed needle-like leaves when first acclimating to a new tank, when handled roughly, or when exposed to sudden water changes — this 'needle drop' is normal and stops once the plant adjusts. Regular trimming controls runaway growth and prevents it from blanketing the surface and shading lower plants. Cross-source notes: Tropica (primary) covers a specific Tropica clone (021A) and is light on numeric parameter data; numeric ranges are drawn from Flowgrow and corroborating hobby guides. Minimum tank volume of 40 L is a guideline for a small bunch; no source specified a strict minimum.
breedingNotes: Propagation is trivial: cut a side shoot or trim a stem section and let it float, anchor, or replant — any segment quickly grows into a new plant. Fragmentation is the standard hobby method; the plant rarely flowers and seeds in home aquariums.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Primary source. The Tropica search landing page (the URL supplied by the controller) returns navigation only; the actual species page was located via search and used here. Tropica entry covers the 021A clone and provides light/CO2 demand, growth rate, height and care level but no specific water-parameter numbers.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature range, floating/anchored placement, rootless biology, algicidal nutrient-competition effect and fry-cover benefit.

additional.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (family Ceratophyllaceae, order Ceratophyllales), cosmopolitan native range, temperature range (4–30 °C; 10–30 °C optimal), pH 6–9, kH 5–24, growth rate, propagation, and explicit confirmation that the plant 'produces a natural algae growth inhibitor'.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature range (10–30 °C), beginner care level, very-fast growth (3–10 cm/week), floating preference, easy propagation by trimming, cold-tolerance for unheated tanks and outdoor ponds. Substituted for Buce Plant which returned 404 for this species.
